core_mechanism: reduction of a doubly-periodic system's partition function to the determinant of a Fourier-diagonalized linear (transfer) operator, whose characteristic zero set is a plane algebraic curve, so that the free-energy density is the Ronkin function of that curve, its Legendre dual gives the surface tension, and the curve's amoeba maps out the phase diagram
brief_reason: The abstract computes surface tension and local Gibbs probabilities from the spectral curve of the Kasteleyn operator, obtaining the surface tension as the Legendre dual of that curve's Ronkin function and reading the phase diagram off the curve's amoeba.
